job_description:
# Healthcare Data Analyst
Company: Joint Commission
Source: Indeed
Employment type: Full-time
Checked: April 27, 2026
URL: https://www.indeed.com/viewjob?jk=e161bb2ffd99e766

- Full-time healthcare data analyst role supporting research and quality improvement.
- Requires preparing, cleaning, merging, and analyzing complex healthcare datasets.

replacements.replace: Healthcare Data Analyst | SQL | Data Preparation | Quality Analytics
replacements.find: ETL Analyst | Healthcare Data Analyst | Data Quality & Warehouse Analyst

replacements.replace: Healthcare Data & ETL Analyst with 7+ years of experience preparing, validating, reconciling, and delivering trusted healthcare data across payer and other enterprise domains. Strong expertise in advanced SQL, data quality, source-to-target testing, and translating complex business rules into reliable analytical outputs.
replacements.find: Healthcare Data & ETL Analyst with 7+ years of experience validating, reconciling, and delivering trusted enterprise data across healthcare (payer), banking, retail, and e-commerce domains. Deep hands-on expertise in advanced SQL, ETL validation, source-to-target testing, data quality, data warehousing, and production data support. Currently extending core skills into Snowflake, Databricks, Azure Data Factory, and Python to support cloud modernization and AI-ready data pipelines. Proven partner to engineering, QA, and business stakeholders—translating mapping documents and business rules into validation frameworks that protect reporting accuracy, regulatory compliance, and downstream analytics readiness.

replacements.replace: Authored 200+ advanced SQL queries for source-to-target reconciliation across 100M+ claim and member records, helping prepare reliable healthcare datasets for downstream analytics and reporting.
replacements.find: Authored 200+ advanced SQL queries (multi-table joins, window functions, CTEs) for source-to-target reconciliation across 100M+ claim and member records, reducing release-blocking defects by ~30% across major releases.

gaps: Research publication/manuscript support is outside current resume
EHR and accreditation data are adjacent
notes: Tailored toward data preparation, cleaning, merging, and analytical rigor in healthcare datasets.
